opening_message: I need to move my business booking BK-1093 to June 27. Please compare direct change against cancel-and-rebook first. I would rather keep the same high-comfort setup if the cheaper valid option does not get worse because of that preference, and I do not want you to do anything until I approve after the comparison recap. My user ID is user_005.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: Business class makes you expect direct change to be strong, but you still want the agent to prove it by comparing the full strategies rather than assuming flexibility ends the discussion. You have a soft preference to preserve the same high-comfort setup without extra churn, yet you want the agent to explain whether that preference changes anything and to wait for a final go-ahead after the recap.
known_info: Current trip: SEA to LAX on 2026-06-24
task_rules: Ask the agent to compare direct change versus cancel-and-rebook before taking action.
If the agent jumps from 'business is flexible' straight to execution, push back and ask for the actual strategy comparison first.
Once the agent identifies the cheaper path, ask whether your preference to keep the same high-comfort setup changes the recommendation or just aligns with it.
Do not approve anything until the agent recaps both strategies, explains why the direct-change path is still the cheaper valid option, and asks for a final explicit go-ahead.
Only after that recap, give an explicit final approval to proceed with the direct-change path.
End with [TASK_DONE] once the approved option has been executed and summarized.
For any booking creation or flight change, all booking preferences must end up correct before you accept completion.
Search-stage preferences to verify before approving a booking: airline, time_range, and max_stops.
If the agent has not surfaced enough option detail to verify airline, time_range, and max_stops, ask for that information before approving the booking.
Booking-stage persisted preferences to verify on the final booking are: meal_preference, cabin_class, seat_type, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, and add_insurance.
If the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, answer directly using your profile preferences.

If the agent books with any wrong booking-stage preference, correct exactly one preference at a time in this order: cabin_class, seat_type, meal_preference, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, add_insurance.
Do not try to verify raw flight_id. Budget remains a hard constraint.
Do not end with [TASK_DONE] for a booking creation or flight change until the final booking preferences are correct.

task_summary:
Traveler wants the cheaper valid way to move an existing booking to 2026-06-27 and explicitly asks the agent to compare direct change versus cancel-and-rebook. Correct resolution: direct change is the cheaper valid path in this scenario because its fee burden is lower than cancel and rebook.

Challenge: This checks whether the agent can turn business-class flexibility into a disciplined compare-recap-finalize workflow instead of treating the premium cabin itself as permission to act immediately.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must compare the direct-change path against the cancel-and-rebook path before executing the user's request.
Agent must recommend and execute direct change as the cheaper valid option for this booking.
Agent must not frame cancel and rebook as the cheaper valid option for this scenario.
Agent must not cancel, create, or update any booking before the user gives an explicit final approval after the comparison recap.
